concerns.text: Task T027 implements a Linear Mixed-Effects Model (LMM) using statsmodels, directly violating FR-005 which mandates 'Wilcoxon Signed-Rank tests'. This is a semantic weakening of the statistical requirement, changing the core methodology from a non-parametric paired test to a parametric hierarchical model without spec amendment.

Task T029a instructs the implementer to 'update spec.md SC-006' if the precision threshold is not met. This violates the principle of fixed success criteria; a task cannot retroactively alter a defined Success Criterion (SC-006) to accommodate failure. The task should report the failure, not rewrite the requirement.

FR-007 requires the system to 'calculate the classifier's precision and recall' on the ground truth subset. Task T017 only covers the 'selection' and 'saving' of the subset. The calculation logic is missing from the US1 phase, creating a gap in the deliverable for FR-007. The calculation is deferred to T029a (US3), breaking the immediate verification loop required by FR-007.

Task T028a documents Benjamini-Hochberg assumptions in 'docs/paper/methodology.md'. However, the Constitution (Principle IV) and Plan (Single Source of Truth) require that all figures/statistics trace back to code/data, not hand-typed documentation. While documentation is needed, the task description implies a manual documentation step that risks decoupling the methodology from the actual code implementation, violating the 'Single Source of Truth' constraint if the code and doc diverge.
